examples: The Giralda minaret is the masterpiece of Almohad architecture.
The explosion occurred in a low income neighborhood, near a wholesale flower market and a major heritage site: the 800-year-old Qutub minar, the world's tallest brick minaret.
Built of white marble that shimmers in both sunlight and moonlight, the Taj Mahal stands on an eighteen-foot-high marble platform which has a delicate minaret on each corner.
One reason, say churchmen, is that a minaret on the skyline might mislead first-time visitors.
The glistening white marble and the swirling, minaret-like towers never fail to impress.
